description: Every Eventbrite organizer endpoint, plus a local SQLite mirror of your events, orders, and attendees you can search offline — the cross-event search Eventbrite removed, restored over your own data.

tools.name: balance_events_remaining-balance
tools.description: Given an organization id and an event id returns an event-level balance specific to the time the endpoint is called. Required: public_organization_id, public_event_id.

tools.name: categories_category-by-id
tools.description: Gets a `category` by ID as ``category``. Required: id. Returns the CategoriesCategoryByIdResponse.

tools.name: categories_list-of
tools.description: Returns a list of Category as categories, including subcategories nested. Returns a paginated response.

tools.name: discounts_delete-a
tools.description: Delete a Discount. Only unused Discounts can be deleted. Warning: A Discount cannot be restored after being deleted. Required: discount_id. Returns the DiscountsDeleteAResponse. Destructive.

tools.name: discounts_retrieve-a
tools.description: Retrieve a Discount by Discount ID. Required: discount_id. Returns the DiscountsRetrieveAResponse.

tools.name: discounts_update-a
tools.description: Update a Discount by Discount ID. Required: discount_id. Optional: discount. Returns the new DiscountsUpdateAResponse.

tools.name: event_canned-questions_delete-default-question-by-id
tools.description: Deactivate a canned question for the event. Required: event_id, question_id. Returns the CannedQuestionsDeleteDefaultQuestionByIdResponse. Destructive.

tools.name: event_canned-questions_get-default-question-by-id
tools.description: Retrieve a Canned Question by Event and Question ID. Required: event_id, question_id. Returns the CannedQuestionsGetDefaultQuestionByIdResponse.

tools.name: event_canned-questions_update-default-question-by-id
tools.description: Modify details of the canned question of the event. Required: event_id, question_id. Returns the new CannedQuestionsUpdateDefaultQuestionByIdResponse.

tools.name: events_delete-an
tools.description: Delete an Event if the delete is permitted. Returns a boolean indicating the success or failure of the delete action. To delete an Event, the Event must not have any pending or completed orders. If the event is a series parent, all series occurrences must be in a valid state to be deleted. Deleting the series parent will delete all series occurrences. Required: event_id. Destructive.

tools.name: events_retrieve-an
tools.description: Retrieve an Event by Event ID. > **Note**: If the Event being retrieved was created using the new version of Create, then you may notice that the Event’s description field is now being used to hold the event summary. To retrieve your event’s fully-rendered HTML description, you will need to make an additional API call to retrieve the Event's full HTML description. Required: event_id. Returns the EventsRetrieveAnResponse.

tools.name: events_update-an
tools.description: Update Event by Event ID. Note that if the event is a series parent, updating `name`, `description`, `hide_start_date`, `hide_end_date`, `currency`, `show_remaining`, `password`, `capacity`, or `source` on the series parent will update these fields on all occurrences in the series. Required: event_id. Optional: event. Returns the new EventsUpdateAnResponse.

tools.name: events_attendees_list-by-event
tools.description: List Attendees by Event ID. Returns a paginated response. Required: event_id.

tools.name: events_attendees_retrieve-an
tools.description: Retrieve an Attendee by Attendee ID. Required: event_id, attendee_id. Returns the AttendeesRetrieveAnResponse.

tools.name: events_cancel_an-event
tools.description: Cancel an Event. Returns a boolean indicating the success or failure of the cancel action. To cancel an Event, the Event must not have any pending or completed orders. If the event is a series parent, all series occurrences must be in a valid state to be canceled. Canceling the series parent will cancel all series occurrences. Required: event_id.

tools.name: events_canned-questions_create-a-default-question-for-an-event
tools.description: Create a default Question for an Event. Returns the result as a `question` array. Required: event_id. Optional: Headers, question.

tools.name: events_canned-questions_list-default-questions-by-event
tools.description: List default Questions by Event ID. Returns a paginated response. Required: event_id.

tools.name: events_capacity-tier_retrieve-a
tools.description: Retrieve the capacity tier for an event. Required: event_id. Returns the CapacityTierRetrieveAResponse.

tools.name: events_capacity-tier_update-a
tools.description: Update the capacity tier for an event. Partial updates are supported. Submit only attributes that are changed. These rules must apply when updating capacity tier: - Sum of `quantity_total` from capacity holds cannot exceed remaining capacity quantity - If the event is already oversold, new remaining capacity quantity cannot become further negative (oversold) To **create GA capacity hold inventory tiers** for an event, include payload with `holds`. `capacity_total` must be supplied if event does not have a capacity set. For example: ```json { 'capacity_total': 100, 'holds': [ { 'name': 'Marketing', 'quantity_total': 10, 'sort_order': 1, }, ] } ``` To **update/delete GA capacity hold inventory tiers** for an event, include payload with `holds` that contain `id` and fields of the hold tier to be updated. Deleting is the same as updating with `is_deleted` set to `true`. Deleting a hold tier that has tickets sold or associated with attendees will be denied. `quantity_total` for a hold tier cannot be reduced below the quantity used (`quantity_sold` + `quantity_pending`). Payload can contain partial data/partial hold inventory tiers to be updated/deleted. For example: ```json { 'holds': [ {'id': 'I987', 'name': 'Marketing'}, # update existing tier {'id': 'I988', 'is_deleted': true}, # delete existing tier {'name': 'Accounting', 'quantity_total': 10}, # create new tier ] } ```. Required: event_id. Optional: capacity_tier. Returns the new CapacityTierUpdateAResponse.

tools.name: events_copy_an-event
tools.description: Copy the Event, creating a duplicate version of the Event with a new Event ID. Use to create a new Event based on an existing Event. Returns the Event object for the newly created Event. The Event payment options, payout method, refund policy, and tax settings will copy to the new Event. Required: event_id. Optional: end_date, name, start_date (plus 2 more).

params.name: end_date
params.description: 01-31T13:00:00Z (datetime, optional) - The end time of the new event

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the new event

params.name: start_date
params.description: 01-31T13:00:00Z (datetime, optional) - The start time of the new event

params.name: summary
params.description: The summary of the new event

params.name: timezone
params.description: Timezone for the new event (Olson format)

tools.name: events_description_retrieve-full-html
tools.description:
Returns the fully rendered description for an Event as a string of HTML. This endpoint will work with events created using New or Classic Create. ```json { 'description: Example summary!n

My event's description would go here.

' } ``` #### Permissions `event.details:read`. Required: event_id.

tools.name: events_display-settings_retrieve
tools.description: Retrieve the Display Settings for an Event by Event ID. Required: event_id. Returns the DisplaySettingsRetrieveResponse.

tools.name: events_display-settings_update
tools.description: Update Display Settings for an Event by Event ID. Required: event_id. Optional: display_settings. Returns the new DisplaySettingsUpdateResponse.

tools.name: events_inventory-tiers_delete-an
tools.description: Mark an existing Inventory Tier as deleted. Required: event_id, inventory_tier_id. Returns the InventoryTiersDeleteAnResponse. Destructive.

tools.name: events_inventory-tiers_list-by-event
tools.description: Retrieves inventory tiers for an event. Required: event_id. Returns the InventoryTiersListByEventResponse.

tools.name: events_inventory-tiers_retrieve-an
tools.description: Retrieve an Inventory Tier by ID for an Event. Required: event_id, inventory_tier_id. Returns the InventoryTiersRetrieveAnResponse.

tools.name: events_inventory-tiers_update-an
tools.description: Update an existing Inventory Iier by ID for an Event. Partial updates are supported. Submit only attributes that are changed. Required: event_id, inventory_tier_id. Optional: inventory_tier. Returns the new InventoryTiersUpdateAnResponse.

tools.name: events_inventory-tiers_update-multiple
tools.description: Update multiple existing Inventory Tiers for an Event. Partial updates are supported. Submit only attributes that are changed. Required: event_id. Optional: inventory_tiers. Returns the new InventoryTiersUpdateMultipleResponse.

tools.name: events_orders_list-by-event-id
tools.description: List Orders by Event ID. Returns a paginated response. Required: event_id.

tools.name: events_publish_an-event
tools.description: Publish an Event. In order for publish to be permitted, the event must have all necessary information, including a name and description, an organizer, at least one ticket, and valid payment options. This API endpoint will return argument errors for event fields that fail to validate the publish requirements. Returns a boolean indicating success or failure of the publish. If the event is a series parent, all occurrences in the series must be in a valid state to be published. Publishing the series parent will publish all series occurrences. Deleted Events can not be published. Required: event_id.

tools.name: events_questions_create-a-custom-for-an-event
tools.description: Create a custom Question for an Event. Returns the result as a `question` array. Required: event_id. Optional: Headers, question.

tools.name: events_questions_delete-a-custom-for-an-event
tools.description: Delete a custom Question by Event and Question ID. Required: event_id, question_id. Returns the QuestionsDeleteACustomForAnEventResponse. Destructive.

tools.name: events_questions_get-custom-by-id
tools.description: Retrieve a Custom Question by Event and Question ID. Required: event_id, question_id. Returns the QuestionsGetCustomByIdResponse.

tools.name: events_questions_list-custom-by-event
tools.description: List custom Questions by Event ID. Returns a paginated response, with a `question` array. Required: event_id.

tools.name: events_schedules_create-an-event
tools.description: Creating an event schedule requires that a series parent event has already been created. For instructions on how to create a series parent event, please refer to the [Create Event API](#events-create-api). Creating an event schedule will add occurrences to a series parent event, according to the pattern specified in the schedule. Each occurrence is backed by an [Event](#event_object), and will be associated with the series parent event specified in the request. If an occurrence specified by the schedule has the same date and time as an existing occurrence, the new occurrence is ignored and not created (the rest of the occurrences specified by the schedule are still created). Required: event_id. Optional: schedule. Returns the new SchedulesCreateAnEventResponse.

params.name: event_id
params.description: Event ID. Must be a valid series parent event.

tools.name: events_seatmaps_create-seat-map-for-event
tools.description: Create a Seat Map for a new reserved seating event by copying an existing seat map. Event must be a new reserved seating. Once a seat map is already created for the event, this endpoint will return error. [List Seat Maps by Organization](#list_seat_maps_for_organization) can be used to find source seat maps and their IDs. Required: event_id. Optional: source_seatmap_id. Returns the new SeatmapsCreateSeatMapForEventResponse.

params.name: source_seatmap_id
params.description: m1 (string, required) - ID of source Seat Map to copy data from.

tools.name: events_structured-content_retrieve-latest-published-version-of-by-event-id
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated response of structured content for the given event `id`. This will retrieve the latest `published` version of the structured content. Therefore you must publish a version of the content before using this endpoint - aka send in `publish=true` with current set of modules to [Set Structured Content](#set-structured-content-by-event-id-and-version) endpoint. It will default to getting the structured content of the event listing page if no purpose is provided. Required: id. Returns the StructuredContentRetrieveLatestPublishedVersionOfByEventIdResponse.

tools.name: events_structured-content_retrieve-latest-working-version-of-by-event-id
tools.description: Retrieve a paginated response of structured content for the given event `id`. This will retrieve the latest `working` version of the structured content - this means latest version that is either published or unpublished. It will default to getting the structured content of the event listing page if no purpose is provided. Required: id. Returns the StructuredContentRetrieveLatestWorkingVersionOfByEventIdResponse.

tools.name: events_structured-content_set-by-event-id-and-version
tools.description: Add new structured content to the event `id` for a specific `version` of structured content. This endpoint encapsulates both create and update for structured content. It will default to creating/updating the structured content for the listing page if no purpose is provided. Make sure to send in `publish=true` with current* set of modules. If you do not publish your structured content, then your most updated structured content will not be visible to public users looking at your event listing page - it will only be visible to you. From a technical perspective, [Get Latest Working Version of Structured Content by Event Id](#get-latest-working-version-of-structured-content-by-event-id) will return your most recent modules (published or unpublished), but [Get Latest Published Version of Structured Content by Event Id](#get-latest-publish-version-of-structured-content-by-event-id) will return only the latest structured content that has been published. * If you ONLY send in `publish=true` without the current set of modules, it will not work - modules will return as empty. Make sure to send in your most current modules AND `publish=true`. Required: id, version. Optional: access_type, modules, publish (plus 1 more). Returns the new StructuredContentSetByEventIdAndVersionResponse.

params.name: publish
params.description: Boolean indicating whether to publish contents after saving.

tools.description:
Retrieve Ticket Buyer Settings by Event ID. Example: ```json { 'confirmation_message': { 'text': '

Confirmation Message

', 'html': 'Sales Ended Message' }, 'survey_info': { 'text': 'Filling in this survey is required to attend the event', 'html': 'Filling in this survey is required to attend the event' }, 'event_id': '43253626762', 'survey_name': 'Registration Page Title', 'refund_request_enabled': true, 'allow_attendee_update': true, 'survey_time_limit': 15, 'redirect_url': null, 'survey_respondent': 'attendee', 'survey_ticket_classes': ['125', '374'] } } ```. tools.description: Create a new Ticket Class. > **Note:** After **May 7, 2020**, we will require you to provide an **inventory_tier_id** as part of your request for any **ticket_classes** you are creating or updating for a tiered event. For more information refer to our [changelog](https://www.eventbrite.com/platform/docs/changelog). > **Add-On creation:** First you will need to create an [Add-On Inventory Tier](https://www.eventbrite.com/platform/api#/reference/inventory-tiers/create-an-inventory-tier) with **count_against_event_capacity** set to false and then provide the **inventory_tier_id** of the Add-On Inventory Tier when creating a new Ticket Class as part of your request. Required: event_id. Returns the new TicketClassesCreateATicketClassResponse.

tools.name: events_unpublish_an-event
tools.description: Unpublish an Event. Returns a boolean indicating the success or failure of the unpublish action. To unpublish a free Event, including a past free Event, the Event must not have any pending or completed orders. A completed and paid out paid Event can be unpublished. A paid Event that is not completed and paid out can only be unpublished if the Event does not have any pending or completed orders. If the event is a series parent, all occurrences in the series must be in a valid state to be unpublished. Unpublishing a series parent will unpublish all series occurrences. A series occurrence cannot be unpublished individually. Required: event_id.

tools.name: organizations_events_create-an
tools.description: Create a new Event. By default, this API creates an event that occurs once. In order to create a series of events with multiple occurrences (also known as a 'repeating event' or 'recurring event'), you must first create one event to serve as the 'series parent', then add occurrences to the series parent. Creating the series parent is done by calling the Create Event API with the `is_series` attribute set to `True`. Occurrences can then be added to the newly created series parent, using the [Event Schedule API](#schedules-create-api). Required: organization_id. Optional: event. Returns the new EventsCreateAnResponse.

params.name: organization_id
params.description: ID of the Organization that owns the Event.

tools.description: List Events by Organization ID. Returns a paginated response. Required: organization_id.

tools.name: organizations_members_list-of-an-organization

In case we don't send the locale we have 2 scenarios: * If event_id is provided the event's locale will be used * For other cases default locale will be used. Required: organization_id, strings. Optional: event_id, locale, venue_id. Returns the new TextOverridesCreateResponse.

Returns the new TicketGroupsCreateAResponse.

tools.name: pricing_calculate-items
tools.description: Calculates the Fees that Eventbrite would charge for a given price as it’s shown on the ticket authoring flow. This price would be hypothetical, as the pricing calculation would be based on the passed parameters instead of facts as it happens when an order is created. This price is a simplified view. The price reported can’t be used to calculate the price of an order. Its used to get fees, taxes and total price depending from scope parameter. The scope can be as one of the members: `organization`, `event`, `ticket_class` or `assortment_plan`. Depending on the scope type, the scope identifier has different meanings: For scope.type ``organization`` scope.identifier represents an organization id. For scope.type ``event`` scope.identifier represents an event id. For scope.type ``ticket_class`` scope.identifier represents a ticket class id. For scope.type ``assortment_plan`` scope.identifier can take a value of either 'package1' or 'package2' Returns a `item_pricing` according to the provided base price and scope. Required: base_price, country, scope. Optional: absorb_fees, absorb_taxes, channel (plus 1 more).

params.name: absorb_fees
params.description: If set to true, the Fees will be included in the base price provided instead of adding them on top of it. False by default.

params.name: absorb_taxes
params.description: If set to true, the Taxes will be included in the base price provided instead of adding them on top of it. False by default.

params.name: base_price
params.description: The base price for the calculation.

params.name: channel
params.description: Sales channel used to calculate the price. If it’s not provided, web will be used by default.

params.name: country
params.description: ISO 3166 2-letter country code where the event would be payed out.

params.name: payment_type
params.description: The payment type used to calculate the price. If it’s not provided, eventbrite will be used by default.

params.name: scope
params.description: The scope for which the item calculations will be made.

tools.name: pricing_list
tools.description: List all available Pricing rates. Returns a paginated response. Required: country, currency. Optional: plan, payment_type, channel (plus 1 more).

params.name: country
params.description: Filter Fee Rates by country ISO 3166 alpha-2 code.

params.name: currency
params.description: Filter Fee Rates by currency ISO 4217 3-character code.

params.name: plan
params.description: Filter Fee Rates by Assortment package name. If it’s not provided, or the value is ‘any’, all the existing variants will be returned.

params.name: payment_type
params.description: Filter Fee Rates by the payment type. If it’s not provided, or the value is ‘any’, all the existing variants will be returned.

params.name: channel
params.description: Filter Fee Rates by the sales channel. If it’s not provided, or the value is ‘any’, all the existing variants will be returned.

params.name: item_type
params.description: Filter Fee Rates by the item type. If it’s not provided, or the value is ‘any’, all the existing variants will be returned.

tools.name: reports_retrieve-a-attendee
tools.description: Retrieve an Attendee Report by Event ID or Event status. Returns array of ReportsRetrieveAAttendeeItem.

tools.name: reports_retrieve-a-sales
tools.description: Retrieve a sales Report by Event ID or Event status. Returns array of ReportsRetrieveASalesItem.

tools.name: series_retrieve-an-event
tools.description: Retrieve the parent Event Series by Event Series ID. Required: event_series_id. Returns the SeriesRetrieveAnEventResponse.

tools.name: series_events_list-by-series
tools.description: List Events by Event Series ID. Returns a paginated response. Required: event_series_id.

tools.name: subcategories_list-of
tools.description: List all available Subcategories. Returns a paginated response.

tools.name: subcategories_subcategory-by-id
tools.description: Retrieve a Subcategory by Subcategory ID. Required: subcategory_id. Returns the SubcategoriesSubcategoryByIdResponse.

tools.name: ticket-groups_delete-a
tools.description: Delete a Ticket Group. The status of the Ticket Group is changed to `deleted`. Required: ticket_group_id. Returns the TicketGroupsDeleteAResponse. Destructive.

tools.name: ticket-groups_retrieve-a
tools.description: Retrieve a Ticket Group by Ticket Group ID. Required: ticket_group_id. Returns the TicketGroupsRetrieveAResponse.

tools.name: ticket-groups_update-a
tools.description: Update Ticket Group by Ticket Group ID. Required: ticket_group_id. Optional: ticket_group. Returns the new TicketGroupsUpdateAResponse.

tools.name: users_list-your-organizations
tools.description: List the Organizations to which you are a Member. Returns a paginated response.

tools.name: users_retrieve-information-about-a-account
tools.description: Returns a user for the specified `user` as user. If you want to get details about the currently authenticated user, use /users/me/. To include the User’s assortment package in the response, add the assortment expansion parameter: /users/me/?expand=assortment. Required: user_id.

tools.name: users_retrieve-information-about-your-account
tools.description: Retrieve Information About Your User Account. Returns the UsersRetrieveInformationAboutYourAccountResponse.

tools.name: users_orders_list-by-user-id
tools.description: List Orders by User ID. Returns a paginated response. Required: user_id.

tools.name: users_organizations_list-by-user
tools.description: List Organizations by User ID. Returns a paginated response. Required: user_id.

tools.name: venues_retrieve-a
tools.description: Retrieve a Venue by Venue ID. Required: venue_id. Returns the VenuesRetrieveAResponse.

tools.name: venues_update-a
tools.description: Update a Venue by Venue ID. Required: venue_id. Optional: venue. Returns the new VenuesUpdateAResponse.

tools.name: venues_events_list-by-venue
tools.description: List Events by Venue ID. Returns a paginated response. Required: venue_id.

tools.name: webhooks_create-deprecated
tools.description: Create a Webhook. > Warning: Access to this API will be no longer usable on June 1st, 2020. For more information regarding deprecated APIs, refer to our [changelog](https://www.eventbrite.com/platform/docs/changelog). Returns the new WebhooksCreateDeprecatedResponse.

tools.name: webhooks_delete-by-id
tools.description: Delete a Webhook by ID. Required: id. Returns the WebhooksDeleteByIdResponse. Destructive.

tools.name: webhooks_list-of-deprecation
tools.description: List Webhooks. > Warning: Access to this API will be no longer usable on June 1st, 2020. For more information regarding deprecated APIs, refer to our [changelog](https://www.eventbrite.com/platform/docs/changelog). Returns the WebhooksListOfDeprecationResponse.
